title: pelargonium × caryophyllaceum
intro: Pelargonium × caryophyllaceum, also known as Rose Geranium, is a hybrid plant species within the Geraniaceae family, derived from the cross between Pelargonium capitatum and Pelargonium fasciculatum. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as geraniol and linalool. The health benefits of Rose Geranium include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of menopause, as well as its potential to aid in weight loss and improve skin health. In herbal preparations, Rose Geranium is often used in teas, essential oils, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
